_provenance: Schema-derived fixture with hand-computed R-U reference values for the data-starved case. Pool constructed manually with N=60, giving k=floor(0.05*60)=3 distinct tail observations. fractional_weight = 0.05*60 - 3 = 0.0 (no atom partial contribution). See m2_cvar_known_pool.derivation.md for the R-U formula. Non-circular: reference values computed from closed form, not from the SUT.

_notes.pool_design: N=60. k=floor(0.05*60)=3 (exactly, fractional_weight=0.0). 3 values strictly below VaR, 1 atom at VaR, 56 above. When fractional_weight=0 the atom contributes ZERO to the CVaR. n_tail_distinct=3 (strictly below only). This is the data-starved case that proves M2 surfaces n=3 to the operator.
_notes.purpose: Proves that M2 displays the genuine small-n tail count (3) to the operator and uses it as the stderr denominator — not the resample count. A small pool (N=60) with only 3 tail observations is 'data-starved' for CVaR estimation purposes.

pool_tail_5pct._note: k=floor(0.05*60)=3. fractional_weight=3.0-3=0.0 so the atom at sorted_pool[3]=-0.015 contributes zero weight. n_tail_distinct=3 (the 3 values strictly below VaR).

expected._cvar_derivation: R-U: (1/0.05)*(1/60)*(-0.06+(-0.04)+(-0.02)+0*(-0.015)) = 20*(1/60)*(-0.12) = -0.04. Equivalent to mean of k=3 below-VaR values because fractional_weight=0.

discriminating_power: n_tail_distinct=3 (not 5000). An implementation using the resample count would give stderr = std/sqrt(5000) = 0.011547/sqrt(5000) = 0.0001633, a 70x understatement. The test asserts the displayed value matches the n=3 value.
